evals.name: command-surface-restores-existing-commands
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit active command surface. Describe the user-facing commands. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list /tk:spec, /tk:gap, /tk:reflect, /tk:handoff, and /tk:meta-feedback as active user-facing commands. Should explain that handoff and meta-feedback were preserved existing commands, not newly invented commands. Should explain that spec/gap write branch-local working memory, reflect creates durable insight, handoff creates continuation context, and meta-feedback extracts generalized TigerKit improvement feedback from session history.
expectations: Treats handoff as restored existing continuation command
Treats meta-feedback as restored existing session-history improvement command

evals.name: gap-single-execution
evals.prompt: Evaluate the /tk:gap command surface. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should describe /tk:gap as a single /tk:gap execution. Should not describe lite and strict as active user-facing quality modes. Should say --lite and --strict are compatibility flags only that do not change quality gates if mentioned. Should describe analysisDepth as direct, bounded, expanded, or exhaustive-capped. Should preserve existing /tk:gap command and not create a new skill or remove an existing skill.

evals.name: gap-analysis-depth-heuristic
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap analysis depth selection. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should choose direct only for one explicit local surface with no API/DTO/state/auth/payment/data mutation/shared component/ambiguity trigger. Should choose bounded for single screen/component/command with nearby 1-depth usage lookup or representative samples up to 3. Should choose expanded for shared component, design-system, API/DTO/state transition, source conflict risk, inaccessible reference, ambiguous Product/API/Design decision, or divergent similar implementations. Should choose exhaustive-capped for P0/P1 candidate, auth/permission/payment/data mutation/destructive action, release gate, or cross-module impact. Hard triggers should run before riskScore tie-breakers. Should record analysisDepth, depthReasons, riskScore, sideEffectConfidence, verificationEscalation, compatibilityFlags, dispatchPlan, dispatchSkips, baselineAutoRefreshGate, claimFreshnessGate, and heuristicProof.analysisDepth with cumulativeBaseline, refreshed origin/main iterationBaseline, currentScore, cumulativeImprovementRatio, iterationImprovementRatio, score direction, and claimAllowed true before claiming depth improvement. Explicit --analysis-depth should not lower the heuristic-required minimum depth for risky surfaces; lower requested depth should be escalated and recorded. Should not call the choice lite or strict mode.
expectations: Requires analysisDepth cumulativeBaseline refreshed iterationBaseline currentScore ratios and claimAllowed true
Hard triggers run before riskScore tie-breakers

evals.name: gap-performance-proof
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap performance proof requirements for the single /tk:gap execution. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should require a measured performance proof rather than a vague estimate. Should use the contract-derived critical path proxy when runtime wall-clock instrumentation is unavailable. Should require speed proof to record cumulativeBaseline, refreshed origin/main iterationBaseline, currentScore, cumulativeImprovementRatio, iterationImprovementRatio, score direction lower_is_better, performance.agentCriticalPathGroups, performance.deterministicStageGroups, performance.runProcedureSteps, performance.measurementMethod, baselineAutoRefreshGate, baseline-snapshot.json, and heuristicProof.speed in the run receipt or manifest. Current optimized contract target should show cumulative baseline 87.1, iteration baseline 50.3, currentScore <= 50.3, cumulativeImprovementRatio = 87.1 / 50.3 = 1.731... with display ratio 1.73, and iterationImprovementRatio = 50.3 / 50.3 = 1.00 with no new iteration speed improvement claim by contract-derived-critical-path-proxy. Should calculate baseline and current with the same proxy weights. Credited dispatchSkips may count only when credited, criticalPathDelta, and evidenceCoveragePreserved are recorded. Should not claim success from phrases such as expected, estimated, or likely.
expectations: Requires measurementMethod and critical path components
Requires cumulative and refreshed origin/main iteration speed baselines
Requires credited dispatch skip fields before counting skip benefit
Requires cumulative and iteration improvement ratios with explicit formula

evals.name: gap-consumer-fallback-not-producer-absence
evals.prompt: Evaluate a /tk:gap candidate that claims an API does not provide a required value, but cites only frontend fallback/default/mapper evidence. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not accept the candidate as P0/P1/P2 and should not create SourceConflict from consumer-only evidence. If API contract, schema, endpoint response, serializer, data model, backend fixture, or owner-confirmed producer behavior is provided or discoverable in the repo, it should inspect that producer surface before asking the user. Should reject or downgrade with missing_producer_evidence, missing_evidence, or unverifiable only after available producer surfaces are checked. Should state producer-side evidence is required, tell the user what producer evidence was checked and what is still missing, and create a ClarificationNeeded confirmation request when the missing producer evidence blocks the gap decision.
expectations: Checks provided or discoverable producer evidence before asking user
Uses missing_producer_evidence only after available producer surfaces are checked
Does not accept SourceConflict without producer evidence
Creates ClarificationNeeded for missing producer evidence when decision-blocking

evals.name: gap-ambiguity-blocks-judge-accept-path
evals.prompt: Evaluate a /tk:gap candidate where Product/API/Design source priority is ambiguous. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not silently choose the likely interpretation. Should route the item to SourceConflict or ClarificationNeeded before accepted finding path. Should continue other clear checks. Should record evidence, impact, recommendation, and status for the clarification.

evals.name: gap-final-receipt-single-execution
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap stdout receipt for a completed run. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should output analysis depth, expansion reasons, verification escalation, dispatch skip summary, Candidate Intake Gate summary, BaselineAutoRefreshGate status, ClaimFreshnessGate status, heuristic proof summary with cumulative and refreshed iteration FP/FN/speed/depth ratios, report path, P0/P1/P2 counts, source conflict count, clarification-needed count, rejected/downgraded count, numeric performance proof, and compact Actionable Findings and Rejected/Downgraded tables using short run-local Ref values instead of long canonical Candidate/Finding IDs. Should not output executed preset, recommended preset, or Strict executed as primary labels.
expectations: Includes dispatch skip and Candidate Intake Gate summaries
Includes heuristic proof summary with cumulative and iteration FP/FN/speed/depth ratios
Compact tables use short Ref values instead of long canonical IDs

evals.name: meta-feedback-uses-session-history-for-gap-improvements
evals.prompt: You are evaluating /tk:meta-feedback after a session where the user said gap was slow, backend findings had false positives, mode wording was confusing, and ambiguous UI/API decisions should be resolved with the user. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should use the session history as evidence, generalize the feedback without exposing project assets, and propose TigerKit command or skill improvements. It should capture performance friction for gap analysis depth, dispatch gate needs, candidate evidence precision gates for stale file:line/module-path coordinates, producer-absence evidence gates for backend false-positive reduction, ambiguity consent gate guidance, and UI clarification via tables or aligned TUI/ASCII mockups. It should not create repo rule patches like reflect and should not output raw user quotes, paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels.
expectations: Uses session history as the evidence source
Generalizes candidate evidence precision gate improvement
Mentions tables or aligned TUI/ASCII mockups for UI clarification
Proposes TigerKit command or skill improvement
Does not expose raw user quotes
Does not expose paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels
Does not behave like /tk:reflect repo rule patching

evals.name: handoff-remains-continuation-command
evals.prompt: You are evaluating /tk:handoff after a branch has spec and gap artifacts. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should preserve /tk:handoff as the existing continuation command. It should write .claude/handoffs/current.md by default, include Reader Guide and Resume Prompt, optionally mention latest branch-local Spec Patch and Gap Run paths when observed, and create a dated archive only when archive=true or an explicit archive request is present.
expectations: Can reference latest branch-local Spec Patch and Gap Run paths
Does not create dated archive without archive=true or explicit archive request

evals.name: reflect-applies-directly-to-rules-and-claude
evals.prompt: You are evaluating /tk:reflect after branch-local gap runs produced durable repo-wide insight candidates. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should default to apply=true, read branch-local TigerKit memory, write directly to CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/* depending on target scope, treat --dry-run and --apply=false as preview-only aliases, avoid modifying source code, dedupe repeated insights, and avoid creating a separate tigerkit-reflections sidecar file.
expectations: Does not create tigerkit-reflections sidecar file

evals.name: gap-false-negative-coverage-proof
evals.prompt: Evaluate a /tk:gap run that could miss a P0/P1 issue because target surfaces or skipped agents were not checked. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should require TargetSurfaceCoverageGate, DispatchCompletenessGate, and ClaimFreshnessGate before claiming false-negative improvement. TargetSurfaceCoverageGate should record checked contract surfaces, target/plan/producer surfaces, and unchecked required surfaces for the selected analysisDepth. DispatchCompletenessGate should compare SourcePresenceManifest with dispatchPlan and record falseNegativeRisk for skipped agents. CriticalRedTeamAgent should perform one missed-P0/P1 targeted search within the selected depth scope. heuristicProof.falseNegative should include metric, denominator, cumulativeBaseline, iterationBaseline, currentScore, cumulativeImprovementRatio, iterationImprovementRatio, improvement formula, and claimAllowed, and claimAllowed should be false if required surfaces are unchecked, dispatch completeness is missing, or proof inputs are stale.
expectations: Runs missed P0/P1 targeted search once
Blocks false-negative claim when required coverage is missing

evals.name: gap-combined-heuristic-proof-claim
evals.prompt: Evaluate whether /tk:gap may claim cumulative and per-iteration improvement for false-positive reduction, false-negative reduction, speed, and analysis depth. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should require heuristicProof.cumulativeRequiredImprovementRatio = 1.3 and heuristicProof.iterationRequiredImprovementRatio > 1.0. For falsePositive, falseNegative, speed, and analysisDepth, should require metric, scoreDirection, cumulativeBaseline, iterationBaseline, currentScore, cumulativeImprovementRatio, iterationImprovementRatio, improvementFormula, and claimAllowed. Higher-is-better metrics should use currentScore / baselineScore; speed should use baselineScore / currentScore. Should require claimFreshnessGate status, checkedAfter, staleInputs, and repairedInputs as a separate claim gate rather than a score denominator. Should allow the combined claim only when all four cumulative ratios meet the cumulative target, all four iteration ratios are > 1.0, all four claimAllowed values are true, and ClaimFreshnessGate passes. Should reject the combined claim if fixed cumulative baseline alone is used for a new iteration claim, consumer-only producer evidence, unchecked target surfaces, missing dispatch completeness, uncredited dispatch skips, risk-score-before-hard-trigger depth selection, or stale proof inputs are used.
expectations: Requires RequirementTraceabilityGate and SeverityScopeGate in FP proof
Requires ClaimFreshnessGate as separate claim gate
Rejects combined claim if any cumulative or iteration subproof is below threshold
Rejects fixed cumulative baseline used as iteration proof
